help.troubleshootingSub: Quick fixes for the most common hiccups
help.tsWifiTitle: Wi-Fi is slow or won't connect
help.tsWifiBody: Check you're on the "Schuur80" network (password: Schuur80) — the QR code on the Wi-Fi page joins it in one tap. Move closer to the living area if the signal is weak in a far corner. Still nothing? The owners next door can restart the router in a minute.
help.tsColdBody: Check the radiator valve in that room is open, and give the underfloor heating time — the barn's thick walls warm up slowly but hold heat well. Doors to unused rooms are best kept closed. The heating page in the House section explains the controls; the owners can adjust the main system if needed.
help.tsProjectorTitle: The cinema projector shows no image
help.tsProjectorBody: Give the projector 30–60 seconds after switching on — the lamp warms up slowly. Check the right input source is selected and that the streaming device is awake (press any button on its remote). The projector page in the House section has step-by-step instructions.
help.tsHotTubBody: Heating a hot tub takes several hours, so keep the cover on whenever it's not in use — that's what holds the heat. If you're planning an evening soak, check the temperature in the morning. Operating details are being confirmed by the owners; they're right next door and happy to get it ready for you.
help.tsPowerBody: First check if the neighbours' lights are also off (a local outage usually resolves itself). If it's just the barn, a fuse has probably tripped — the owners next door know the fuse box and will sort it quickly. Keep a phone torch handy; country nights are dark.

arrival.title: Arrival
arrival.weatherPending: Live weather will be shown here when you're online.

wifi.scanHint: Point your phone's camera at this code — a "Join Schuur80" prompt appears. Tap it and you're online, no typing needed.
wifi.instructionsIos: On iPhone: open the Camera app, aim at the QR code and tap the "Join Schuur80 network" banner. Or copy the password above, then go to Settings → Wi-Fi → Schuur80 and paste it.
wifi.instructionsAndroid: On Android: open the Camera (or Google Lens), aim at the QR code and tap the connect prompt. Or copy the password above, then open Settings → Network → Wi-Fi → Schuur80 and paste it.
wifi.instructionsDesktop: On a laptop: choose the Schuur80 network in your Wi-Fi settings and paste the password copied above. On your phone, simply scan the QR code with the camera.
wifi.qrPendingBody: As soon as the owners confirm the network details, a QR code will appear here that connects your phone automatically.

onboarding.welcomeBody: Your guest companion for the barn — the house, the garden, the animals and everything around, in your pocket.
onboarding.installTitleIos: Add it to your home screen
onboarding.iosStep1: Tap the Share button at the bottom of Safari.
onboarding.iosStep2: Choose "Add to Home Screen", then tap Add.
onboarding.androidStep1: Tap the ⋮ menu in the top corner of Chrome.
onboarding.desktopStep1: Look for the install icon at the right end of the address bar.
onboarding.desktopStep2: Click it and confirm to install the app.
onboarding.worksInBrowser: Prefer not to install? No problem — everything works right here in the browser, even offline after your first visit.

printCard.hint: Print this card at A6 size (or A5 for a larger version) and place it on the kitchen island or by the entrance.
printCard.appQrAlt: QR code that opens the Schuur 80 guest app
printCard.appQrCaption: Everything about the barn — rooms, appliances, the animals, tips and help — in 7 languages.
printCard.wifiQrAlt: QR code that connects to the Wi-Fi
printCard.wifiQrCaption: Point your camera at the code and tap the prompt — connected, no typing.

ownerq.title: Questions for the owners
ownerq.intro: Dear owners — this page gathers everything the app still needs from you. Every answer replaces a placeholder with confirmed information for your guests.
ownerq.footer: Answers can be given in any order and in your own words — short notes are enough. They will be worked into the app in all seven languages.
